To find articles or browse research databases, use the library's Databases A-Z list. It's the authoritative index of every database the library subscribes to -- organized alphabetically and by subject -- and links directly into each database with proper authentication.

Why send you there instead of looking up a specific database name? Database names overlap and shift ("Web of Science" vs "Web of Knowledge", "PsycINFO" vs "Psychology and Behavioral Sciences Collection"); the A-Z page disambiguates them with current names + subject tags.

Databases A-Z: https://libguides.lib.miamioh.edu/az/databases

Miami University Libraries’ “Regional Libraries” include the Gardner-Harvey Library (Middletown campus) and the Rentschler Library (Hamilton campus) [1]. The Regional Libraries page lists their locations and phone numbers: Gardner-Harvey Library, 4200 N. University Blvd., Middletown, Ohio 45042, P: (513) 727-3222; and Rentschler Library, 1601 University Blvd, Hamilton, Ohio 45011, P: (513) 785-3235 [1].

On the Oxford campus, King Library’s reading rooms are open and arranged for safe physical distancing [1]. The room-reservations page also points to study spaces in King, where you can reserve study/collaboration spaces online [2].
I started to answer but couldn't verify my sources. Rather than send you something I can't back up, please ask a librarian through Ask Us.
I don't have a reliable answer to that. You can ask a librarian directly through Ask Us.

Book a study room for Middletown through the reservation page at https://muohio.libcal.com/reserve/middletown [1]. That page is the Middletown room availability/reservation system for Gardner-Harvey [1][2].
